visualizations.title: From Watching to Reporting a Number
visualizations.description: How an observation turns into a measurement, and why uncertainty always tags along

visualizations.title: Reporting a Measurement Honestly
visualizations.description: Every measurement should carry its uncertainty, and error is measured relative to the true value

steps.explanation: A good measurement isn't just a number - it's a number plus how sure you are of it.
This tells you how far off (accuracy), as a percentage of the true value.

visualizations.title: A Measuring Instrument
visualizations.description: A simple ruler-shaped instrument, rotating so you can see it's a physical tool with a fixed smallest marking

visualizations.title: Accuracy vs. Precision
visualizations.description: Repeated measurements scatter near a target: tight and centered is both accurate and precise; tight but off-center is precise but not accurate

visualizations.title: Repeated Measurements Converge
visualizations.description: Averaging more trials pulls your measured value closer to the true value of 10 m

visualizations.title: No Measurement Is Perfect
visualizations.description: A quick recap of measurement, accuracy, and precision

spec.body_markdown:
## Measurement & Observation

**Observation** is watching what happens. **Measurement** is putting a number on it.

No measurement is perfect:

- **Accuracy** - how close you are to the true value
- **Precision** - how exact (how many decimal places) your measurement is

A scale can be precise (65.4 kg) without being accurate (if you actually weigh 65.37 kg... or 68 kg).

Limits to watch for: instrument limits (your ruler's smallest marking), human error (misreading a scale), environmental factors (temperature, vibration), and at tiny scales, the act of measuring can change what you're measuring.

Good scientists always report the uncertainty, not just the number.

visualizations.title: Instrument Precision Lab
visualizations.description: A true length of 10.00 m is being measured with a ruler of adjustable precision - see how the reading rounds off

visualizations.title: What Limits a Measurement
visualizations.description: The main sources of measurement uncertainty
